one_time_description: ISC capital contributions for Health Centre of Excellence ($47,700,721) and ISC COVID domes ($2,431,174) recognized above the operating surplus line
economic_notes: Government business partnerships include Bison Container Homes LLP (51% owned, modular homes), Kici Sipi Communications LP (50% owned, broadband infrastructure), and Tower Camp (52% owned, remote accommodations). Playgreen Development Corporation provides civil and site works. Norway House Pharmacy LP operates pharmaceutical services. Kinosao Sipi Business Development Corporation undertakes economic development projects.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Large ISC capital contributions of $50,131,895 (Health Centre of Excellence $47,700,721 + COVID domes $2,431,174) are recorded separately below the operating surplus line, significantly inflating total surplus. The entity consolidates approximately 20 subsidiaries including incorporated and unincorporated entities. Three government business partnerships (Bison Container Homes, Kici Sipi Communications, Tower Camp) are accounted for using the modified equity method. Tangible capital assets grew significantly from $227M to $291M due to major Health Centre of Excellence capital project ($48M additions in HCE). Construction in progress of $125.3M represents a substantial portion of TCA. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($111,528,257) + CMHC ($4,819,652) + Province of Manitoba ($3,091,396) + Assembly of Manitoba Chiefs ($809,046) + MKO ($2,337,616) - some of these may be pass-through. No remuneration schedule was included in the extracted text. Net debt position of -$63.1M improved from -$73.3M prior year.
